questions.question: What does the 'head' of a first-order rule typically represent?
questions.explanation: The head of a rule specifies the conclusion that is derived when the conditions are met.

questions.question: In a first-order rule, what is the function called that combines the head and body?
questions.explanation: The implication is the function that combines the head and body of a rule to derive a conclusion.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a first-order rule in machine learning?
questions.explanation: First-order rules are used to derive conclusions based on the conditions specified in the rule.

questions.question: What is an example of a first-order rule?
questions.explanation: An example of a first-order rule is 'If (P and Q) then (R', where P, Q, and R are predicates.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a first-order rule and a higher-order rule?
options: A) A first-order rule has a simpler predicate
B) A first-order rule has a more complex predicate
C) A first-order rule has no predicate
D) A first-order rule has a different function
questions.explanation: The main difference between a first-order rule and a higher-order rule is that a first-order rule has a predicate that combines the head and body, while a higher-order rule has a predicate that combines multiple rules.
